message:
[LOW] fcp-sandbox: close double-canonicalize TOCTOU in WASI preopens

prepare_preopened_dir (wasi.rs:1261) was calling canonicalize twice
against the same declared path — once via resolve_policy_path inside
ensure_preopen_path_is_stable, and again directly at the tail of the
function before returning the PathBuf to wasmtime. Two separate
canonicalize syscalls open a TOCTOU window: an attacker with write
access to a path ancestor can swap a regular directory for a symlink
in between, and the second canonicalize follows the link — widening
the guest mount to the attacker's chosen target.

The stability check in 7c28cbc0 closed the "preopen declares a
pre-existing symlinked ancestor" case; this commit closes the
companion race where the symlink is injected AFTER the stability
check passes.

Two changes:

1. ensure_preopen_path_is_stable now returns WasiResult<PathBuf>
   carrying the already-canonical resolved form. Callers consume that
   directly instead of re-canonicalizing.

2. For writable paths, where create_dir_all walks the declared path
   component-by-component and can be raced mid-creation, re-verify
   stability AFTER create_dir_all and refuse if the post-create
   resolved form differs from the pre-create prediction.

The window between the stability check and wasmtime's eventual open
cannot be closed at this layer without openat-style fd passing
(wasmtime doesn't currently expose that); eliminating the extraneous
canonicalize syscall is the narrowest correct improvement.

Single-caller API change (prepare_preopened_dir); readonly path
behavior is behaviorally unchanged. cargo check -p fcp-sandbox green.

Note: two pre-existing NoOp sandbox test failures surfaced in the
pass (test_noop_sandbox_verify_file_access and _verify_all_operations
in sandbox.rs), independent of this change — the NoOp tests exercise
a different code path from WASI preopens. Not investigated here; a
separate fcp-crypto WIP on disk blocks a clean re-test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
